questionText: The random variable $X$ is modelled by $X \sim N(25, 3^2)$. (a) Write down the median and the mode of $X$. (b) Find $P(X > 25)$.

steps.explanation: The variable is normal with mean $\mu = 25$ and standard deviation $\sigma = 3$. The curve is a symmetric bell centred on the mean.

steps.description: Recall the key property of the normal curve
steps.explanation: Because the normal curve is symmetric and single-peaked, its centre of balance (mean), its middle value (median) and its highest point (mode) all coincide at $\mu$.
Half the area lies on each side of the mean, so the median — the value with $50\%$ below it — is exactly $25$.
The peak of the bell sits directly above the mean, so the most likely region is around $25$ and the mode is $25$.
The value $25$ is the mean itself, and the curve is symmetric about the mean, so the area to its right equals the area to its left.
Since the two halves are equal and together make $1$, each half is exactly $0.5$.
These follow purely from the symmetry of the normal distribution, with no standardising needed.
